questions.question: To total revenue by region, which fields go where in a PivotTable?
questions.explanation: Categories you list down the side go in Rows; the numbers you summarise go in Values.

questions.question: How do you turn a column of order dates into months and quarters in a pivot?
options: Right-click a date → Group → tick Months/Quarters/Years
questions.explanation: Right-click any date in the pivot → Group rolls up by Year/Quarter/Month. It needs real dates (Stage 1).

questions.question: Which feature shows each region's share of total revenue as a percentage?
options: Value Field Settings → Show Values As → % of Grand Total
questions.explanation: Show Values As → % of Grand Total converts the sum into a percentage of the whole.

questions.question: How do you make one slicer control several PivotTables at once?
options: Right-click the slicer → Report Connections → tick the pivots
Copy the slicer onto each pivot
questions.explanation: Report Connections links one slicer to multiple pivots — one click filters them all. The core trick behind a simple dashboard.

questions.question: To build a Top-5 rep leaderboard from a pivot of rep revenue, you…
options: Right-click a rep → Filter → Top 10 → set to 5, then sort Largest to Smallest
Add a slicer for each rep
questions.explanation: Value Filter → Top 10 (set to 5) plus a Largest-to-Smallest sort gives a clean top-5 leaderboard.

questions.question: You added new orders to the Sales table but the pivot hasn't changed. What do you do?
options: Right-click the pivot → Refresh (or Data → Refresh All)
questions.explanation: Pivots cache their data — Refresh pulls in the latest. Because Sales is a Table, new rows are included automatically on refresh.
